text: Mutation of land records: When land ownership changes (by sale, inheritance, or gift), the new owner must apply for mutation (name change) in revenue records at the local Tehsil/Taluk office, submitting sale deed or succession certificate, within the state-prescribed time limit (varies by state, often 3-6 months).

Land dispute between neighbors: If a boundary dispute arises, the first step is to request a government land survey (measurement) from the local Tehsildar or Revenue Inspector to establish boundaries per official records. If unresolved, a civil suit for declaration of title and injunction can be filed in the civil court under the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act (RFCTLARR), 2013: Governs compensation when government acquires private land for public purpose. Compensation is generally 2x to 4x the market value depending on rural or urban location, plus solatium.

Tenancy rights on agricultural land: Various state Tenancy Acts (e.g. state-specific Land Reforms Acts) protect long-term tenant farmers' rights to continue cultivation and, in some states, provide a path to ownership after a specified period of continuous cultivation. Rules vary significantly by state.
